ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general congee entry; no specific Vietnamese chicken and cabbage congee entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers rice porridge/congee broadly; no Vietnamese-specific chicken-cabbage variant code exists. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Vietnamese dish combination.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one bowl of approximately 350 g, comprising a portion of rice porridge alongside a cabbage and shredded chicken salad dressed with fish sauce, lime, and chili.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken And Cabbage Salad With Rice Porridge (Gỏi Cháo Gà Bắp Cải) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (cr_id G00010576). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the fat profile of the dish components (chicken skin, fish sauce dressing) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; value estimated at approximately 7.5 g/100g based on typical Vietnamese chicken congee composition per USDA FoodData Central analogous entries.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Rice porridge / congee analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Gỏi Cháo Gà Bắp Cải. GI of 78 is assigned based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for rice porridge/congee (GI range 78–90 for white rice congee, prolonged cooking). The lower bound of 78 is used as a conservative estimate given the mixed dish context (protein and vegetable components may modestly attenuate the glycaemic response). Glycemic load calculated as: GI × carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 78 × 20.65 / 100 ≈ 16.1, using 350 g serving size and 5.9 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 810 mg/100g — well above the 600 mg/100g threshold; potassium at 64 mg/100g is insufficient to offset the sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; if consumed, request reduced fish sauce in both the dressing and the congee, avoid additional table condiments, and limit portion to no more than 150 g to keep sodium intake below 1215 mg per occasion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Rice porridge (congee) has a very high glycemic index (GI ~78–90) due to prolonged cooking that extensively gelatinises starch, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid rice porridge as the base of this dish; if consumed, limit to a small portion (100–150 g), pair with additional high-protein and high-fibre accompaniments to blunt the glycaemic spike, and monitor blood glucose closely after eating.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Fat content is 44.54 g/100g with estimated saturated fat of 7.5 g/100g; sodium at 810 mg/100g further elevates cardiovascular risk through blood pressure elevation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to its high total fat, elevated saturated fat, and very high sodium content; if consumed, request preparation with reduced oil and fish sauce, choose a small portion, and avoid pairing with other high-sodium or high-fat foods in the same meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium at 810 mg/100g worsens RA-related tissue oedema; chili (nightshade) may trigger joint inflammation; dietary fibre is 0 g/100g providing no gut microbiome benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request chili-free preparation, reduced fish sauce, and increased cabbage proportion to leverage sulforaphane anti-inflammatory properties; ginger in the dressing provides beneficial gingerols and should be requested in greater quantity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken and Cabbage Salad with Rice Porridge (Vietnamese: Gỏi Cháo Gà Bắp Cải) is a Vietnamese dish combining two components: a warm, slow-cooked white rice congee (cháo) and a cold shredded chicken and cabbage salad (gỏi) dressed with fish sauce, lime juice, chili, ginger, and fried shallots. The congee base is made by simmering white rice in chicken broth until the grains fully break down into a smooth, thick porridge with a mild, savoury flavour. The salad component features poached or shredded chicken breast mixed with thinly sliced cabbage, providing a crunchy, tangy contrast to the soft congee. The dish is served together in a bowl, with the salad placed atop or alongside the porridge. Nutritionally, the HPB Singapore database records this dish at 430 kcal/100g with a very high fat content of 44.54 g/100g (likely reflecting the oil-rich dressing and fried shallots), low protein at 1.32 g/100g, and moderate carbohydrate at 5.9 g/100g. Sodium is very high at 810 mg/100g due to fish sauce. The dish carries a high glycaemic index (~78) from the congee base, making it unsuitable for diabetics and hypertensive individuals without significant modification. It is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Vietnamese cuisine and is found in Vietnamese restaurants in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010576) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The dish is an exact match in the HPB database. Saturated fat estimated and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for rice congee.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (7.5 g/100g) is estimated from the fat profile of typical Vietnamese chicken congee components (chicken skin, fish sauce dressing, fried shallots, cooking oil) using USDA FoodData Central analogous entries, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Vietnamese dish are not available in the HPB Singapore database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB for this dish; this is a confirmed zero per the HPB source, likely reflecting the low-fibre nature of the congee base and the small quantity of cabbage in the as-served proportion. 4. GI of 78 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for white rice congee (GI range 78–90); the lower bound is used conservatively. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = 78 × (5.9 × 3.5) / 100 = 78 × 20.65 / 100 ≈ 16.1. 5. This dish is a Vietnamese dish served in Singapore; multilingual requirements per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md mandate Vietnamese native-script name only (not Singaporean dish rules). Vietnamese name Gỏi Cháo Gà Bắp Cải is provided.
